root_motivation: Ten independent SessionStart hooks emit ~21 KB of raw, unsynthesized text into every Claude session. No tool consolidates these signals into a single prioritized briefing. The developer (Joe Yen) loses orientation time at session open because there is no structured arrival summary — only a firehose of raw dumps from recall, agorabus, git status, self-review docket, and build manifest. The threshold CLI synthesizes these sources into one ≤4 KB briefing so every Claude session starts oriented rather than overwhelmed.
user_persona: Joe Yen running Claude Code sessions on a wintermute laptop; threshold brief is invoked by a SessionStart hook (or manually) to produce an arrival briefing. Also consumed by machine agents via --format json.

acceptance_criteria.description: cargo build and cargo test are green; cargo clippy adds no new warnings over the autobuilder baseline; binary installs to ~/.local/bin/threshold.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac1.rs — cargo build succeeds, cargo test passes, cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings exits 0, binary present at ~/.local/bin/threshold after install

acceptance_criteria.description: threshold brief --format json against a FakeSource fixture set produces a Briefing with the documented sections and a stable, documented JSON schema validated by a test.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac2.rs — runs threshold::briefing::synthesize with FakeSource inputs, asserts JSON output validates against the documented Briefing schema with all required section keys present

acceptance_criteria.description: The synthesizer is a pure function (no I/O in its signature) and is covered by unit tests for: dedup of identical signals, priority ordering, section assignment, and the --max-items cap.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac3.rs — calls synthesize() directly with crafted Vec<Signal> inputs, asserts dedup, ordering, section assignment, and max-items truncation

acceptance_criteria.description: Each real SignalSource degrades to an empty contribution (not an error and not a panic) when its backing data is missing or malformed — proven by a test that points a source at a nonexistent path.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac4.rs — constructs each concrete SignalSource with a nonexistent/malformed path and asserts collect() returns Ok(vec![])

acceptance_criteria.description: threshold brief (text mode) run live on this box produces a non-empty, sectioned briefing under ≤4 KB and completes in < 500 ms cold.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac5.rs — spawns threshold brief with --source-root pointing at a fixture directory, asserts output non-empty, byte count ≤ 4096, wall time < 500ms

acceptance_criteria.description: First line of main() is sigpipe::reset(); threshold brief | head does not panic.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac6.rs — reads src/main.rs and asserts sigpipe::reset() is the first statement; spawns threshold brief | head and asserts exit code is not 141 (SIGPIPE)

acceptance_criteria.description: README documents every source, the JSON schema, and the --source-root testing seam.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac7.rs — reads README.md and asserts presence of all six source names, 'JSON schema' section, and '--source-root' documentation

scope: threshold CLI binary with a 'brief' subcommand
Signal enum/struct with kind, title, body, priority, source, freshness fields
Briefing struct with four documented sections: Mid-flight, Owed to you, Changed since last session, Don't redo
SignalSource trait with six concrete implementations: RecallSource, GossipSource, BuildManifestSource, GitSource, DocketSource, ReviewDueSource
Pure synthesize(signals: Vec<Signal>) -> Briefing function — no I/O
README with source documentation, JSON schema, and testing seam
non_goals: Hook wiring to replace the existing SessionStart hooks (threshold-hook — later PRD)
Network fetches or remote data sources

additional.sigpipe_reset: First line of main() must be sigpipe::reset() — standing lesson from SIGPIPE-panic class

five_whys_trace.q: Why build a new CLI instead of improving the existing hooks?
five_whys_trace.a: The hooks are independent; consolidating in a hook would require one hook to depend on all others completing first, creating ordering and timing problems. A separate CLI is invoked after all hooks complete.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the synthesis need to be a pure function?
five_whys_trace.a: Pure functions are unit-testable without I/O mocking overhead, and the 20+ KB firehose problem is fundamentally a data-transformation problem, not an I/O problem — the sources are already queryable.
4 KB is 1/5 of today's 20,888-byte firehose and fits comfortably in a single terminal screenful. Orientation without scrolling is the functional requirement.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should threshold brief replace the existing hooks or supplement them?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Supplement — the hooks continue to run; threshold brief is called afterward (or manually) to synthesize. Hook replacement is threshold-hook, a later PRD.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Which FakeSource fixture format should the JSON schema test use?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: In-memory FakeSource that implements SignalSource, exercised via threshold's public library API, not via CLI subprocess. The schema test serializes the Briefing struct directly.
